content.problemStatement: You are given a sequence a1,a2,…,aNa_{1}, a_{2}, \ldots, a_{N}a1​,a2​,…,aN​ of non-negative integers. Your task is to process QQQ queries of the following format: each query is described by two integers L≤RL \leq RL≤R and asks to calculate the number of triples (i,j,k)(i, j, k)(i,j,k), such that L<i<j<k<RL < i < j < k < RL<i<j<k<R and aL>ai<aj>ak<aRa_{L} > a_{i} < a_{j} > a_{k} < a_{R}aL​>ai​<aj​>ak​<aR​.
constraints: 1 ≤ L ≤ R ≤ N for each query in the input
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers N and Q, denoting the size of the sequence and the number of queries to process. The second line contains N non-negative integers a1, a2, …, aN. Each of the next Q lines contains two integers L and R, describing the corresponding query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output a single line containing one integer: the number of triples (i, j, k) as described in the problem statement.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first and second queries have 8 valid triplets each. The third query has no valid triplets.
The first and second queries have no valid triplets.
The first query has 6 valid triplets. The second query has 2 valid triplets.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over all possible triples (i, j, k) within the given range [L, R] and checking if the conditions aL > ai < aj > ak < aR are met. This approach involves using three nested loops to generate all possible triples and then applying the given conditions to count the valid triples.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q * N^3) per test case, O(Q * N^3) total. The precomputation is not required in this case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is a constant amount of space used to store the input and the count of valid triples.
